steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The question asks what "manifest" most nearly means in this context. The sun is already down, yet it is "manifest in the livid tongues of fire darting towards the zenith" — it can be seen through those fire-colored streaks of light in the sky. "Manifest" here describes something visible or perceivable even though not directly present.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
The word needs to mean visible or able to be perceived. Substitute each choice:
  • Choice A — Dwindling: means decreasing or fading away — while the fire-light does fade, "manifest" describes how the sun appears, not a rate of decrease. ✗
  • Choice B — Realized: means understood or made real — would suggest the sun is being comprehended mentally, but the passage describes a visual, sensory phenomenon. ✗
  • Choice C — Situated: means located or placed — describes physical position, not the act of being seen or perceived through the fire. ✗
  • Choice D — Perceptible: means able to be perceived or noticed — the sun, though set, is still perceptible through the colored streaks of fire it leaves in the sky. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
